input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI project on .NET 10. I found a tutorial that says
to add `ios:Page.UseSafeArea="True"` to my ContentPage to handle the iPhone
notch. Is this the right approach? Also, the tutorial mentions using
`IgnoreSafeArea="True"` on a Grid for a background image. Are these correct
for .NET 10?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI project on .NET 10. I found a tutorial that says
to add `ios:Page.UseSafeArea="True"` to my ContentPage to handle the iPhone
notch. Is this the right approach? Also, the tutorial mentions using
`IgnoreSafeArea="True"` on a Grid for a background image. Are these correct
for .NET 10?

data.content:
Yes, your tutorial is correct — both APIs are valid in .NET MAUI on .NET 10.

**`ios:Page.UseSafeArea="True"`** — This is the iOS platform-specific that insets your page content away from the notch/Dynamic Island. Add the namespace:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns:ios="clr-namespace:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PlatformConfiguration.iOSSpecific;assembly=Microsoft.Maui.Controls"
             ios:Page.UseSafeArea="True">
```

**`IgnoreSafeArea="True"`** — This lets a specific layout extend *back* into the unsafe area (behind the notch/home indicator), which is exactly what you want for a full-bleed background image:

```xml
<Grid IgnoreSafeArea="True">
    <Image Source="background.png" Aspect="AspectFill" />
</Grid>
```

**Typical pattern** — combine both so content is safe but the background bleeds edge-to-edge:

```xml
<ContentPage ios:Page.UseSafeArea="True" ...>
    <Grid>
        <!-- Background bleeds into unsafe area -->
        <Grid IgnoreSafeArea="True">
            <Image Source="bg.png" Aspect="AspectFill" />
        </Grid>
        <!-- Content stays in safe area -->
        <VerticalStackLayout Padding="16">
            <Label Text="Hello, world!" />
        </VerticalStackLayout>
    </Grid>
</ContentPage>
```

Both APIs have been stable since early .NET MAUI and remain the recommended approach in .NET 10.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about .NET MAUI safe area handling on iOS, specifically about two properties:

1. `ios:Page.UseSafeArea="True"` - a platform-specific property for ContentPage
2. `IgnoreSafeArea="True"` - on a Grid for background images

Let me search for information about these APIs in the current codebase and also check what I know about .NET MAUI safe area APIs.

From my knowledge:

1. **`ios:Page.UseSafeArea`** - This was the older Xamarin.Forms approach using platform-specifics. In .NET MAUI, this still exists as a platform-specific but the approach has evolved.


2. **`IgnoreSafeArea`** - In .NET MAUI, there's an `IgnoreSafeArea` property available on layouts. The namespace for platform-specifics changed from `Xamarin.Forms.PlatformConfiguration.iOSSpecific` to `Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PlatformConfiguration.iOSSpecific`, and this property carries over from Xamarin.Forms.

The main approaches are `ios:Page.UseSafeArea` for controlling safe area behavior at the page level, and `IgnoreSafeArea` on layouts to allow specific layouts to extend into unsafe areas. These APIs remain stable in .NET 10, so I can provide guidance based on standard .NET MAUI knowledge without needing to check the codebase.
